verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass transmembrane protein with its own membrane anchor that reaches the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane, but only after the acrosome reaction—a defined, non-baseline cellular state. Baseline state: sequestered in acrosomal membrane. Post-acrosomal reaction: surface-exposed on equatorial segment. This tissue-restricted (germline/sperm) + cell-state-induced (acrosome reaction) combination classifies as contextual. The protein body is accessible from extracellular space via its own TM mechanism once the acrosome reaction occurs.
